two-sum: Try checking every pair using two nested loops.
Use a hashmap to store previous values for O(n) time.
Try sorting and use two-pointer approach.
valid-parentheses: Try pushing opening brackets into a stack.
Pop and match when you see a closing bracket.
If the stack is empty at the end, it's valid.
merge-sorted-array: Merge both arrays into a new one and sort.
Can you do it in-place from the end?
Start from the end and fill backwards to avoid shifting.
longest-substring-without-repeating-char: Check all substrings and track duplicates.
Can you slide a window while storing visited chars?
Use sliding window and hash set for O(n) solution.
maximum-subarray: Try checking all subarrays and track max sum.
Use a running sum and reset if it goes below 0.
Kadane’s Algorithm gives optimal O(n) result.
climbing-stairs: Try calculating every possible way recursively.
Use bottom-up DP: f(n) = f(n-1) + f(n-2).
reverse-linked-list: Try storing nodes in an array and reversing.
Use 3 pointers to reverse links one by one.
Iteratively update next pointers for O(1) space.
binary-search: Search the array one by one linearly.
Can you divide and search halves recursively?
Use binary search by comparing mid each time.
flood-fill: Try changing all matching color pixels one by one.
Apply DFS or BFS to paint all connected regions.
rotting-oranges: Rot each orange and then check again.
Use BFS to simulate rotting minute by minute.
Track layers (time) in BFS and count fresh oranges.
